audit_result.proof_trace: The clamping logic count.max(validator_custody_requirement).min(number_of_custody_groups) only enforces the invariant when validator_custody_requirement <= number_of_custody_groups, but Config has no validation of this constraint (types/src/config.rs:39 explicitly states Config has no invariants). If a misconfigured network sets validator_custody_requirement > number_of_custody_groups, the function returns number_of_custody_groups, violating the lower bound.
audit_result.attack_scenario: A malicious testnet operator sets validator_custody_requirement=200 and number_of_custody_groups=100 in the network config. Validators with moderate stake (count between 100-200) will be assigned custody requirement of 100 instead of the mandated 200, causing them to subscribe to fewer custody groups than required, potentially breaking PeerDAS data availability guarantees.

PROP-57888860-inv-025.text: Validator custody requirement must be clamped between VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT and NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS.
PROP-57888860-inv-025.assertion: forall get_custody_req(indices) -> r: VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT <= r <= NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS
